annotations.label: Topological space (neighbourhood axioms)

anchor.snippet: The function

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `TopologicalSpace` via open sets; the neighbourhood-axiom construction is available as `TopologicalSpace.mkOfNhds` with companion lemma `nhds_mkOfNhds`, not as the primary definition.

annotations.label: Open set (from neighbourhoods)

anchor.snippet: is defined to be open if

annotations.note: The equivalence `IsOpen s ↔ ∀ x ∈ s, s ∈ 𝓝 x` is the lemma `isOpen_iff_mem_nhds`; Mathlib does not take this as the primitive definition of `IsOpen`.

annotations.label: Neighbourhood system on the real line

anchor.snippet: A standard example of such a system of neighbourhoods is for the real line

annotations.note: The standard topology on `ℝ` is provided as a `TopologicalSpace` instance via its metric/uniform structure; the neighbourhood-system description is not the primary form.

annotations.label: Topology via open sets

anchor.snippet: A topology on a set X may be defined as a collection

annotations.note: `class TopologicalSpace` is defined exactly via a collection of open sets with the three axioms (univ open, binary intersection, arbitrary union).

annotations.label: Closed set

anchor.snippet: is said to be closed in

annotations.note: `IsClosed s` is defined as `IsOpen sᶜ`.

annotations.label: Clopen set

anchor.snippet: is said to be clopen

annotations.note: `IsClopen s := IsClosed s ∧ IsOpen s`.

annotations.label: Trivial (indiscrete) topology

anchor.snippet: the trivial or indiscrete topology on

annotations.note: `IndiscreteTopology` (equivalently the top element `⊤` of the lattice of topologies) records that the topology has only `∅` and `univ` as open sets.

annotations.label: Six-subset topology

anchor.snippet: of six subsets of

annotations.note: Mathlib has no specific entry for this ad-hoc three-point example topology.

annotations.label: Discrete topology / discrete space

anchor.snippet: the discrete topology on

annotations.note: `DiscreteTopology α` records `‹TopologicalSpace α› = ⊥`, the topology in which every set is open.

annotations.label: Finite subsets of integers is not a topology

anchor.snippet: of all finite subsets of the integers plus

annotations.note: No lemma in Mathlib explicitly states that this particular family fails to be a topology.

annotations.label: Topology via closed sets

anchor.snippet: another way to define a topological space is as a set

annotations.note: `TopologicalSpace.ofClosed` builds a `TopologicalSpace` from a family of closed sets satisfying the dual axioms.

annotations.label: Topology via Kuratowski closure axioms

anchor.snippet: by using the Kuratowski closure axioms

annotations.note: Mathlib has `ClosureOperator` and `closure` but no constructor that builds a `TopologicalSpace` from a Kuratowski closure operator.

annotations.label: Topology via nets / accumulation points

anchor.snippet: A topology is completely determined if for every net in

annotations.note: Mathlib develops convergence via filters rather than nets; there is no constructor producing a topology from a net-convergence relation.

annotations.label: Finer and coarser topologies

anchor.snippet: one says that

annotations.note: The `PartialOrder (TopologicalSpace α)` instance defines `t ≤ s` to mean every `s`-open set is `t`-open, i.e. `t` is finer than `s`.

annotations.label: Lattice of topologies on a set

anchor.snippet: forms a complete lattice

annotations.note: Mathlib provides `instance : CompleteLattice (TopologicalSpace α)` with `⊥` discrete and `⊤` indiscrete.

annotations.label: Continuous function between topological spaces

anchor.snippet: between topological spaces is called continuous if

annotations.note: `Continuous f` is defined by `∀ s, IsOpen s → IsOpen (f ⁻¹' s)`.

annotations.label: Continuity via inverse images of open sets

anchor.snippet: is continuous if the inverse image of every open set is open

annotations.note: This is literally the field defining `Continuous` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homeomorphism and homeomorphic spaces

anchor.snippet: A homeomorphism is a bijection that is continuous and whose inverse is also continuous

annotations.note: `Homeomorph X Y` (notation `X ≃ₜ Y`) bundles an `Equiv` with continuous forward and inverse maps.

annotations.label: Category Top

anchor.snippet: which denotes the category of topological spaces

annotations.note: `TopCat` is the bundled category of topological spaces with continuous maps.

annotations.label: Discrete topology example

anchor.snippet: Any set can be given the discrete topology in which every subset is open

annotations.note: Same as the earlier entry: `DiscreteTopology` records that every set is open.

annotations.label: Trivial topology example

anchor.snippet: any set can be given the trivial topology

annotations.note: Same as the earlier entry: the indiscrete topology corresponds to `⊤` in the lattice.

annotations.label: Finite topological spaces

anchor.snippet: Such spaces are called finite topological spaces

annotations.note: `FinTopCat` is the category of finite topological spaces, requiring `Fintype` plus `TopologicalSpace`.

annotations.label: Cofinite topology

anchor.snippet: Any set can be given the cofinite topology

annotations.note: `CofiniteTopology X` is a type synonym for `X` equipped with the topology whose open sets are `∅` and sets with finite complement.

annotations.label: Cocountable topology

anchor.snippet: Any set can be given the cocountable topology

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Filter.cocompact`/`Filter.cofinite` but no `CocountableTopology` type synonym defining this topology.

annotations.label: Lower limit topology

anchor.snippet: The real line can also be given the lower limit topology

annotations.note: No formalization of the Sorgenfrey/lower-limit topology on `ℝ` was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Order topology on an ordinal

anchor.snippet: may be endowed with the order topology generated by the intervals

annotations.note: `Ordinal` is endowed with the preorder/order topology via `instance : TopologicalSpace Ordinal := Preorder.topology Ordinal`.

annotations.label: Natural topology on manifolds and simplicial complexes

anchor.snippet: Every manifold has a natural topology since it is locally Euclidean

annotations.note: Mathlib manifolds (`ChartedSpace`, `IsManifold`) presuppose an ambient `TopologicalSpace` rather than constructing one from a chart atlas; simplicial complexes carry topologies in `AlgebraicTopology` but not as a single uniform construction.

annotations.label: Sierpiński space

anchor.snippet: The Sierpiński space is the simplest non-discrete topological space

annotations.note: `instance sierpinskiSpace : TopologicalSpace Prop` realises the Sierpiński topology on `Prop`.

annotations.label: Subspace topology

anchor.snippet: Every subset of a topological space can be given the subspace topology

annotations.note: `instTopologicalSpaceSubtype` equips `Subtype p` with the topology induced by `Subtype.val`.

annotations.label: Product topology

anchor.snippet: the product can be given the product topology

annotations.note: `instTopologicalSpaceProd` defines the product topology on `X × Y` as the infimum of the topologies pulled back along the projections.

annotations.label: Quotient topology / quotient space

anchor.snippet: A quotient space is defined as follows

annotations.note: `instTopologicalSpaceQuotient` equips `Quotient s` with the coinduced topology, the largest topology making the quotient map continuous.

annotations.label: Metric topology

anchor.snippet: Every metric space can be given a metric topology

annotations.note: Every `PseudoMetricSpace`/`MetricSpace` carries a canonical `UniformSpace` and hence a `TopologicalSpace` instance whose open sets are unions of open balls.

annotations.label: Standard topology on the real line

anchor.snippet: The standard topology on

annotations.note: `ℝ` carries the standard topology coming from its metric instance.

annotations.label: Base / basis for a topology

anchor.snippet: forms a base or basis for the topology

annotations.note: `TopologicalSpace.IsTopologicalBasis s` asserts that `s` is a basis for the topology.

annotations.label: Standard topology on Euclidean space and complex numbers

anchor.snippet: the basic open sets are the open balls

annotations.note: Euclidean spaces (`EuclideanSpace`, `ℂ`) inherit a topology from their metric/normed structure, with open balls forming a basis.

annotations.label: Topological groups, rings, fields, vector spaces

anchor.snippet: This leads to concepts such as topological groups

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsTopologicalGroup`, `IsTopologicalAddGroup`, `IsTopologicalRing`, `IsTopologicalDivisionRing`, and topological vector/module structures via `ContinuousSMul`.

annotations.label: Zariski topology

anchor.snippet: The Zariski topology is defined algebraically on the spectrum of a ring

annotations.note: `PrimeSpectrum.zariskiTopology` defines the Zariski topology on `PrimeSpectrum R`, whose closed sets are zero loci.

annotations.label: Spectral space

anchor.snippet: A space is spectral if and only if it is the prime spectrum of a ring

annotations.note: `class SpectralSpace` axiomatizes the property; the equivalence with being the prime spectrum of a ring is partially captured via `PrimeSpectrum.instSpectralSpace`.

annotations.label: Specialization preorder

anchor.snippet: the specialization preorder (or canonical preorder ) is defined by

annotations.note: `specializationPreorder X` defines the specialization preorder via `x ⤳ y ↔ 𝓝 x ≤ 𝓝 y`.

annotations.label: Topology from a filter

anchor.snippet: If

annotations.note: Mathlib equips `Filter α` with a canonical topology generated by `Set.Iic (𝓟 s)`; this is the topology on the space of filters.

annotations.label: Outer space of a free group

anchor.snippet: Outer space of a free group

annotations.note: Culler–Vogtmann outer space is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topological property

anchor.snippet: A topological property is a property of spaces that is invariant under homeomorphisms

annotations.note: Mathlib has no explicit definition of "topological property" as a homeomorphism-invariant predicate on spaces.
